title: pharbitis cuspidata
intro: Pharbitis cuspidata, also known as the prickly poppy or prickly pharbitis, is a plant species in the morning glory family that is native to Asia. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds, which contain a compound called rotenone, a natural insecticide and antiseptic. The health benefits of Pharbitis cuspidata include its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative properties. In herbal preparations, the seeds and leaves of Pharbitis cuspidata can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal benefits.
